title: Evan Hynes - Climatebase CEO & Co-Founder
body:
## Overview
**Name:** Evan Hynes  
**Current Role:** CEO & Co-Founder, Climatebase  
**Location:** San Francisco, California  
**LinkedIn:** https://www.linkedin.com/in/evanhynes  
**X/Twitter:** @EvanDHynes

## Company: Climatebase
- **Founded:** 2020
- **Mission:** Leading platform for climate careers and solutions
- **Team Size:** ~15 employees
- **Funding:** .3M (Nov 2022) - Lead investors: Climate Capital, Possible Ventures, Shed Capital, Contrarian Ventures, Tapas Capital
- **Description:** Connects job seekers with organizations working on climate solutions; comprehensive job listings, company directories, and resources for climate tech/sustainability sectors

## Professional Background
- **Current (2020-Present):** CEO & Co-Founder, Climatebase
- **2022-Present:** Founder, SF Climate Week  
- **2018-2019:** Founder, Ikiguide (experimental)
- **2017:** Recruiter, Hustle
- **2014-2017:** Head of Community, Make School
- **2014:** Chapter Director & Content Developer, Hackers Of
- **2013-2014:** Account Manager, Oracle (Enterprise Software)
- **2012-2014:** Social Media Specialist, CDC California project
- **2011-2013:** Director, Undie Run Santa Barbara; President, SB Media Group

## Education
- **BA** - Technology Entrepreneurship, Communication, & Fine Art - UC Santa Barbara
- **Technology Entrepreneurship Certificate** - UC Santa Barbara (2011)
- **iOS Development Certificate** - Make School (2016)
- **JavaScript, HTML, CSS Certificate** - First Step Coding (2018)

## Key Achievements
- **Forbes 30 Under 30 - Social Impact (2023)** - Recognized for mobilizing talent towards climate solutions
- Built Climatebase into central hub for climate sector careers
- Connected hundreds of thousands of job seekers to climate organizations
- Founded SF Climate Week and Climate Week Network

## Why Connect
- Climate tech ecosystem leader
- Strong network in sustainability/climate space
- Experience scaling mission-driven platforms
- Background in community building and talent mobilization
- Intersection of tech, entrepreneurship, and climate action

## Notes
- Mission-driven entrepreneur focused on climate solutions
- Leverages technology to address environmental challenges
- Active in climate community building and advocacy
- Based in SF - potential for local connection
